title: Cirrus: enable Fedora 36 aarch64 tasks on EC2
body:
Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

Piggybacks on https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14569

comments.body: @vrothberg any idea whatsup with `Validate fedora-36 {arm64} Build` ?
@lsm5 looks like some kind of connection error?  I'm not sure if it's due to the changes in here or a hiccup?

> @lsm5 looks like some kind of connection error? I'm not sure if it's due to the changes in here or a hiccup?

only thing that comes to mind is something went wrong with aws connection. I'll try changing the encrypted secret.
ha, it was just this stupid `arm64 v/s aarch64` thing for ec2 arch specification. gotta be super careful doing a search and replace.

@lsm5 you're probably going to need to rebase-onto and/or wait for https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14972 as well.  Alternatively, you could **_temporarily_** disable (`skip: $CI == $CI`) all/most non-arm tasks so you can test the new tasks until that PR merges.
`bud podman fedora-36 root host` looks like a redhat registry problem?

@cevich @edsantiago can I get some eyes on `sys podman fedora-36 {aarch64} root host` ? Some command not found, don't know which one. And earlier Ed said `run -127` as indicated in the log suggestion is wrong.

@lsm5 I'm seeing a TON of failures due to the localhost registry being inaccessible.  I'm guessing this is due to the ARM64-image missing or not working?  

Also: 
`# Error: error creating cgroup path /libpod_parent/7c44fa42363ca7d85fc935daa45b5c409ccd125ffe0d0eb56fe8d79116416502: write /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/cgroup.subtree_control: no such file or directory`

That path seems very wrong.  I haven't looked at the test-code, but that strikes me as something is very wrong with the environment (in general).

And:

`podman pull quay.io/libpod/fedora:31`

Best check if that is a manifest-list containing ARM64 image or just a regular x86_64 image.

And:

```
[+1446s] # system_u:system_r:cloud_init_t:s0^@
[+1446s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
[+1446s] # #|     FAIL: SELinux type
[+1446s] # #| expected: 'spc_t'
[+1446s] # #|   actual: 'cloud_init_t'
[+1446s] # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

That's VERY broken.  What the heck is cloud-init doing touching any of this stuff?  cloud-init should run once at VM startup, then get out of the way.  No configuration I'm aware of should cause it to touch anything container-related like this.  Maybe SELinux policy is broken in some way for ARM?
I would guess you have a labeling issue.  Perhaps you need to relabel files if you are moving them around.

> I would guess you have a labeling issue. Perhaps you need to relabel files if you are moving them around.

Superficially sure, but if the test is passing w/o this error on `x86_64`, it suggests a problem specific to the `arm64` environment.  The images are built with substantially the same scripts, so that's why I wonder about a packaging problem.  Note: We **do not** touch `/.autorelabel` in the images, since that would worsen startup time.

> > I would guess you have a labeling issue. Perhaps you need to relabel files if you are moving them around.
> 
> Superficially sure, but if the test is passing w/o this error on `x86_64`, it suggests a problem specific to the `arm64` environment. The images are built with substantially the same scripts, so that's why I wonder about a packaging problem. Note: We **do not** touch `/.autorelabel` in the images, since that would worsen startup time.

i rebased on main and i'm rerunning them now. I'll try getting terminal access on the next failure. 

@cevich RE: packaging problem, you mean, container-selinux / selinux-policy or something else?

The [normal-arch fail](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6329943377313792/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html) is almost certainly flake #10825, a pretty nasty one that's over a year old by now.

The new-arch ones are expected. It will take time to solve or skip those. I'll look into them one by one. If you need to merge this urgently, I suggest filing issues for each one, then adding `skip "FIXME: #12345"` to each one so the 12345s can be tracked.

Correction: `skip_if_aarch64`, which needs to be a new function (easy to write).

Why are the test name (and log files) including curly braces? Is that a deliberate design decision for these tests? It looks odd and seems to break some of my greasemonkey code. (Not a big deal, I can probably fix it, just curious)

> Correction: `skip_if_aarch64`, which needs to be a new function (easy to write).

ack, will check and add
 
> Why are the test name (and log files) including curly braces? Is that a deliberate design decision for these tests? It looks odd and seems to break some of my greasemonkey code. (Not a big deal, I can probably fix it, just curious)

@cevich remind me please why you preferred {aarch64} in curly braces :)

`login` test failures seem to be because the registry image isn't multiarch -- but the 2.8 one is:
```console
$ buildah manifest inspect quay.io/libpod/registry:2.8 | jq -r '.manifests[].platform' 
... amd64, arm, arm64, etc
```
Quick suggestion (which I have no way to test):
```diff
diff --git a/test/system/150-login.bats b/test/system/150-login.bats
index dc902d5fe..b57bb44ab 100644
--- a/test/system/150-login.bats
+++ b/test/system/150-login.bats
@@ -52,7 +52,7 @@ function setup() {                                                                                                                           
     mkdir -p $AUTHDIR
 
     # Registry image; copy of docker.io, but on our own registry
-    local REGISTRY_IMAGE="$PODMAN_TEST_IMAGE_REGISTRY/$PODMAN_TEST_IMAGE_USER/registry:2.7"
+    local REGISTRY_IMAGE="$PODMAN_TEST_IMAGE_REGISTRY/$PODMAN_TEST_IMAGE_USER/registry:2.8"
 
     # Pull registry image, but into a separate container storage
     mkdir -p ${PODMAN_LOGIN_WORKDIR}/root
```

[this one](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5555887191359488/html/sys-podman-fedora-36%20%7Baarch64%7D-root-host.log.html#t--00264) is in code that @cdoern just merged last week:
```
# # podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs pod create --name=resources-cgroupfs --cpus=5 --memory=5m --memory-swap=1g --cpu-shares=1000 --cpuset-cpus=0 --cpuset-mems=0 --device-read-bps=/dev/loop0:1mb --device-write-bps=/dev/loop0:1mb --blkio-weight-device=/dev/loop0:123 --blkio-weight=50
# Error: error creating cgroup path /libpod_parent/e0024c8b8ccc24c247b62a422433c0b69d7c3f930bad3863563fcec0d0db43f1: write /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/cgroup.subtree_control: no such file or directory
# [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```

[this one](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5555887191359488/html/sys-podman-fedora-36%20%7Baarch64%7D-root-host.log.html#t--00291) is systemd, so @vrothberg might be the best person to look at it, but it also could be crun, so, ping, @giuseppe also:
```
# # podman run -d --sdnotify=container quay.io/libpod/fedora:31 sh -c printenv NOTIFY_SOCKET;echo READY;systemd-notify --ready;while ! test -f /stop;do sleep 0.1;done
# 2ff76f9670f13c479196440ac93babe9fc4afa8cbb0e0b6799b73a3b59969292
# # podman logs 2ff76f9670f13c479196440ac93babe9fc4afa8cbb0e0b6799b73a3b59969292
# /run/notify/notify.sock
# READY
# [0;1;31mFailed to notify init system: Permission denied[0m
```
All the permission and SELinux errors make me strongly suspect that SELinux is broken on these systems. It might be that the only way to debug is to ssh into one of them.

@edsantiago what changed in this PR that could trigger a test to fail that passed 3 days ago? these things keep sneaking by CI mysteriously...

@giuseppe WDYT on the subtre_control failure? I have only seen that in rootless since we do not create libpod_parent there.
@cdoern this is a new arch.

> trigger a test to fail that passed 3 days ago?
> @cdoern this is a new arch.

Actually maybe also: The images (AMI's) here (I think) were built *before* the latest `c6013173500215296` ones from last week.  I can't (easily) tell from the ambiguous comment.

Ugh...these unreadable/disconnected AMI ID's are going to drive us crazy.  I'm updating the tags on the AMI to point at the image ID, but there's no visibility of that in Cirrus or at runtime :confounded: We're literally relying on a comment to indicate which build they came from :angry:   I really don't know a good way to make this more foolproof :disappointed:
@edsantiago aarch64 no longer in curly braces. Added skip_if_aarch64 to a couple of tests that failed in the last run. Also, created issue #15074 for that.
I notice you didn't skip any of the SELinux tests. Did that get resolved with the new images?

> I notice you didn't skip any of the SELinux tests. Did that get resolved with the new images?

the last failure only showed the system test failures and nothing else (i guess others were cancelled). I'll see how this run goes.
Next time you rebase, please remove the 761e9f256 commit; and I think you can remove 8d9b7cac2 and 6b5c46735 as well

> Next time you rebase, please remove the [761e9f2](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/761e9f256b7461a8b43b4214f8fa3c66ad22b370) commit; and I think you can remove [8d9b7ca](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/8d9b7cac29ad735b1d65ea57e0164036104c2bc0) and [6b5c467](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/6b5c4673524f565467eb7f00dd9969fd5d2e88d6) as well

@edsantiago do we not need them anymore or do you need the removal to investigate something else?
All those have merged, although in slightly different form than when you rebased on them. The code that merged is Truth, and should not be clobbered by older versions.
repushed and skip_if_aarch64 commented out to check for failure consistency.

> > I notice you didn't skip any of the SELinux tests. Did that get resolved with the new images?
> 
> the last failure only showed the system test failures and nothing else (i guess others were cancelled). I'll see how this run goes.

the system test failures look consistent. It's the same 2 tests failing for remote and non-remote.

```
        _ruamel_yaml.c:6:10: fatal error: Python.h: No such file or directory
            6 | #include "Python.h"
              |          ^~~~~~~~~~
```
Oof, WTF.  Doing some digging...
nonono, that's just because @lsm5 tried removing something, but I guess it can't be removed. Should be easy to just add it back.

> nonono, that's just because @lsm5 tried removing something, but I guess it can't be removed. Should be easy to just add it back.

Our VM images should be uniform across architectures.  Since we're not seeing this error on the x86 tasks, something is clearly not uniform.

Looking in c/automation_images, `cache_images/fedora_packaging.sh` we don't mention `python-devel` anywhere.  I'm taking a quick poke around to see if I can find where it gets brought.

Okay, it looks like the `python-devel` is missing from the regular images too.  If you want to add back in the `dnf install` for those two tasks (Validate and consistency?) I think there's no need to check the architecture, just always install it.  Maybe drop a `TODO` comment suggesting we bundle it into the images for stability.  But I don't see that work as critical ATM.
Opened https://github.com/containers/automation_images/issues/159
@edsantiago @cevich removed that smallcontainer line and added python3-devel back. PTAL once again please, sorry about the PITA :(

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> sorry about the PITA :(

It's no problem bud.
[remote is a flake](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4683338345611264/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--00207) which should go away as soon as #15090 merges.

LGTM but that `resource` flake is going to cause a lot of pain. I foresee an immediate followup PR to `skip` it.

Thanks for your perseverance @lsm5.
/hold cancel
